sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 6.29 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.6)

statement.latex: Suppose that a finite group$A$is isomorphic to the group of all topological automorphisms of a locally compact group$G$. Does there always exist a discrete group whose automorphism group is isomorphic to$A$? This is true if$A$is cyclic; the condition of local compactness of$G$is essential (R.\,J.\,Wille, Indag. Math., 25, no.\,2 (1963), 218--224).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
